text: Everyone feels stressed from time to time, but when the pressure becomes ongoing, it can seriously affect your health.
The strain of too many responsibilities may lead to poor sleep, headaches, and difficulty concentrating.
Recognising the early signs is crucial.
Pay attention to how your body reacts when you feel overwhelmed.
Tense shoulders, a tight stomach, and a racing heart are all common indicators.
Once you identify the triggers, you can take steps to manage them.
Simple habits like walking outdoors, writing in a journal, or talking to a trusted friend can make a profound difference.
It is also important not to neglect your basic needs — eating well, sleeping enough, and taking breaks throughout the day.
Learning to cope with stress is not about eliminating it entirely.
It is about building tolerance so that difficult moments do not define your life.
